definition: collection of qualifiers to specify the meaning of properties and other data element types or of its instanciated values

definition_ml.en: collection of qualifiers to specify the meaning of properties and other data element types or of its instanciated values

note:
NOTE 1 Qualifiers are intended to specify the meaning of properties and other data element types or of its instanciated values. In many cases this specification is essential for the correct interpretation of the value. Example: The value associated during the product life cycle stage “as designed” can well differ from the value during the stage “as built”.
NOTE 2 Association of qualifiers to values is not an activity performed in the dictionary but is rather performed by an application. By providing qualifier objects in the dictionary the application can select terms or values from predefined, standardized terms for qualifiers. Such qualifiers can provide information such as status data or lifecycle data.

definition: qualifier providing a verifiable binding between a data items' representation and a date and time value based on the timescale coordinated universal time (UTC)

definition_ml.en: qualifier providing a verifiable binding between a data items' representation and a date and time value based on the timescale coordinated universal time (UTC)

note:
NOTE 1 A time stamp token may serve different functions:
- to assign to an event, action or data items an unambiguous date and time-value with respect to a common time reference;
- to differentiate events, actions or data items among different points in time with respect to a common time reference.
NOTE 2 A time stamp for the timescale UTC may be produced by the clock of the local computer. This may be sufficient for events or actions limited to the local computer. The local clock of a computer can be easily configured with the result of not being safe, inconsistent and not reliable. This method of time stamping is not suited for interaction with other computers, persons, machinery, etc. on an international level, especially if exact time is a critical issue. For this case the service of a time stamp service provider (e.g. https://www.dfn.de/) is required, which is serving as a common time reference. 
NOTE 3 Time stamping is mandatorily based on a given timescale; here on the coordinated universal time (UTC).
NOTE 4 This property is foreseen to present in the UTC timescale representations of time up to a resolution of 1 ns based on the representation in conformance with ISO 8601-1: 2019. Truncation of positions after decimal sign (of second) is not permitted.

For this case the service of a time stamp service provider (e.g. https://www.dfn.de/) is required, which is serving as a common time reference. 
NOTE 3 Time stamping is mandatorily based on a given timescale; here on the coordinated universal time (UTC).
NOTE 4 This property is foreseen to present in the UTC timescale representations of time up to a resolution of 1 ns based on the representation in conformance with ISO 8601-1: 2019. Truncation of positions after decimal sign (of second) is not permitted.

remark:
REMARK 1 The timescale coordinated universal time (UTC), see IEV 713-05-20, is continuous with discontinuities due to leap seconds, standard time due to summer time and winter time,
REMARK 2 For physical and technical applications, a timescale with quantitative marks is preferred, based on a chosen initial instant together with a unit of measurement.
REMARK 3 Durations or periods needs be measured within the same timescale.

definition_ml.en: qualifier providing a verifiable binding between a data items' representation and a date and time value based on the timescale international atomic time (TAI)

note:
NOTE 1 A time stamp token may serve different functions:
- to assign to an event, action or data items an unambiguous date and time-value with respect to a common time reference;
- to differentiate events, actions or data items among different points in time with respect to a common time reference.
NOTE 2 A time stamp may be produced by the clock of the local computer. This may be sufficient for events or actions limited to the local computer. The local clock of a computer can be easily configured with the result of not being safe, inconsistent and not reliable. This method of time stamping is not suited for interaction with other computers, persons, machinery, etc. on an international level, especially if exact time is a critical issue. For this case the service of a time stamp service provider (e.g. https://www.dfn.de/ ) is required, which is serving as a common time reference. 
NOTE 3 Time stamping is mandatorily based on a given timescale; here on the international atomic time (TAI). It is used in communication devices without having the feature of converting the native bit-based TAI format into the UCT date and time format defined in ISO 8601-1. For such devices the conversion needs be done by an additional display means.
NOTE 4 For information about the bit pattern of the time stamp value (TAI) see IEC 61158-6-10:2019, clause 4.2.1.8.1
